purpose: Canonical disambiguation and normalization rules for experiment situationMask, biomeMask and filterKey during science result generation.
outOfScope: result text style guidance beyond mask/filterKey disambiguation

rules: SituationToken MUST be matched before biome parsing.
If SituationToken is an alias (Flying, InSpace), it represents all underlying situations.
BiomeRemainder is any remaining suffix after situation parsing.

maskValidation.situationMaskRule: A situation is valid if (situationMask & situationBit) != 0
maskValidation.biomeMaskRule: A biome is valid for a situation if (biomeMask & situationBit) != 0
notes: biomeMask refers to the same bit positions as situationMask.
biomeMask = 0 means NO biome-specific results are allowed.

resolutionPolicy.fallbackBehavior: prefer dropping biome over dropping situation

normalizationProcedure: Parse filterKey into bodyName, situationToken, biomeCandidate.
Resolve situationToken into one or more concrete situations (expand aliases if needed).
For each candidate situation: check validity against situationMask.
Discard any situation not allowed by situationMask.
- Check biome existence for the body.
- For each remaining situation, check biomeMask.
- If biomeMask disallows biome: remove biome (keep situation).
Return one normalized key per remaining situation.

priorityOrder: Preserve situation over biome when resolving conflicts
doNot: Do not invent situations not present in situationMask
Do not invent biomes not present in planet database
Do not keep biome-specific flavor when biomeMask disallows it

examples.note: Biome stripped because biomeMask disallows biome-specific results.
